breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION OF REFRACTORY LINING FOR > 1.0 INTRODUCTION
text: 1.0 INTRODUCTION

breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION OF REFRACTORY LINING FOR > 1.0 INTRODUCTION
text:
Bharat Petroleum Corporation Limited (BPCL) Mumbai has planned a revamp of the
existing CDU3 heater (F-101) to implement reliability enhancement recommendations at
BPCL Mumbai refinery.
A few mechanical modifications are envisaged in the fired heater for this capacity
enhancement, which are described elsewhere in the tender. Due to these mechanical
modifications, a few associated refractories also need to be relined. This specification
outlines the technical requirements for refractory relining for the contractor.
The contractor shall prepare refractory drawings, procure refractory materials & anchors,
carry out refractory application, and provide supervision, quality control services, and do
testing of the refractory samples. Contractor shall ensure mobilization of all resources in
Contractor’s scope of supply, manpower, tools , and tackle at the site to complete the job
within the shutdown period.

breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION OF REFRACTORY LINING FOR > 2.0 LIST OF EXPECTED REFRACTORY JOBS
text: 2.0 LIST OF EXPECTED REFRACTORY JOBS

breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION OF REFRACTORY LINING FOR > 2.0 LIST OF EXPECTED REFRACTORY JOBS
text: Heater-wise minimum refractory relining requirements in various areas are identified below. This requirement has been firmed up based on the mechanical modification envisaged. However, there could be other areas found out during the shutdown after opening the heater and dismantling the existing refractory, where refractory relining will be necessary. Refractory thickness and materials shall be as per the table. However, the contractor shall verify the same with the existing, and if any difference shall be brought to the notice of the owner for clarification, except for Type IV insulating castable, which has been replaced by Type V. Heater Area Refractory Remarks Refractory lining to be used work/relining for revamp Radiant Localized floor As per the requirement 125 mm type I castable (cold floor refractory around face) +75 mm type V castable + the burner and 75 mm type I fire brick (hot face) spigot. Lining around the new access door. Radiant CF lining around the 150 mm CF module (1425 C wall tube inlet & outlet. grade, 180 kg /m3) at hot face + 0.1 mm SS316 foil between module and blanket + 25 mm CF ARCH CF Lining around blanket type I A (1260 C grade, the tube support 128 k/m3) CDU 3 Convection Localized refractory Dismantling and Type V castable (50 mm) heater around the new relining (if required) (F-101) jump over in the header box Localized refractory Dismantling and 100 mm type V castable in ETS (around

breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION OF REFRACTORY LINING FOR > 2.0 LIST OF EXPECTED REFRACTORY JOBS
text: 128 k/m3) CDU 3 Convection Localized refractory Dismantling and Type V castable (50 mm) heater around the new relining (if required) (F-101) jump over in the header box Localized refractory Dismantling and 100 mm type V castable in ETS (around the relining (as per future row) requirement) Refractory in As per the requirement 100 mm type V castable new/modified ETS (Localized repair may be required) Localized Dismantling and 175 mm type III castable (with refractoriness relining anchor inside sleeve) around the new ITS. Refractory lining in As per the requirement Type V castable (50 mm) the new / modified (Localized repair may header box be required) APH Localized refractory As per the requirement As per the details in the approved in ducts (at the drawing interface with the new APH)

breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION OF REFRACTORY LINING FOR > 4.0 TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
text: 4.0 TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION OF REFRACTORY LINING FOR > 4.0 TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
text: a) Contractor shall build complete scaffolding to provide an appropriate approach to all work locations in the heaters. b) Contractor shall dismantle the refractory in the areas where refractory relining is envisaged as per the list of work provided in the expected refractory work list. c) Refractory lining around the Arch tube hangers’ location shall be removed on all sides, and the lining shall be relined. d) Contractor shall prepare new module arrangement drawings showing stud spacing, module orientation, etc., for the heater arch & wall, around new tube hanger locations and tube inlet & outlet positions. Bolted-type modules may be considered at locations where installation would be difficult. e) There are locations in the convection section where new tubes could be inserted in the future rows and/or a new provision for tubes needs to be created. In case of a new provision for convection coils, the existing refractory lining of the wall is to be removed 500mm more from both ends of the new tubes. i) In case new ITS needs to be installed in convection, it is required to remove the surrounding refractory to a reasonable extent so that casting could be performed using shuttering. ii) In case the existing steam lancing is to be closed in convection, the refractory is to be installed in that location after providing SS310 retainer plates all around. f) It is envisaged that header boxes may also be constructed new or partially.

breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION OF REFRACTORY LINING FOR > 4.0 TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
text: the existing steam lancing is to be closed in convection, the refractory is to be installed in that location after providing SS310 retainer plates all around. f) It is envisaged that header boxes may also be constructed new or partially. Normally header box castable is found to be very fragile after prolonged usage. Therefore, it will be prudent to change the complete header box castable if it is not usable during shutdown. g) Floor refractory around burners and the new access door should be relined if damaged. Contractor shall restrict the use of heavy machines for breaking, which may lead to cracks/loosening of refractory in large areas.

breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION OF REFRACTORY LINING FOR > 4.0 TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
text: h) Wherever the casing plate is opened up, the same shall be cleaned so that no deposits shall adhere to the casing i) Any perforation and/or thinning of casing plate more than the specified limit, if observed, shall be brought to the knowledge of the owner for appropriate decision before installation of new lining. j) Inside casing plate, in contact with CF lining, shall be painted with epoxy phenolic paint (one coat primer + two final coats) with a total DFT of 225 microns. k) Contractor shall carry out a detailed inspection after entering the heater during shutdown. Any refractory damage identified during shutdown in any location of the heater shall be brought to the notice of the owner and shall be rectified in consultation with the owner. l) Contractor shall also prepare an application procedure. Separate dry out for floor/convection castable (for minimal application) is not required. However, initial heating of the heater shall be slow (~15°C/hr) and 12-24 hours holding would be required at 260° and 550°C. Detailed procedure to be prepared by the contractor. m) Contractor shall procure all refractory/ancillary materials and do application at the site. Refractory material shall be as per the respective EIL standard, and a compliance datasheet shall be obtained from the manufacturer. n) Refractory material shall be procured from EIL approved vendor list. o) Refractory drawings and documentation to be reviewed before procurement and application. p) In general, in the event of

breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION OF REFRACTORY LINING FOR > 4.0 TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
text: and a compliance datasheet shall be obtained from the manufacturer. n) Refractory material shall be procured from EIL approved vendor list. o) Refractory drawings and documentation to be reviewed before procurement and application. p) In general, in the event of cut out to be made in the casing plate with refractory lining, the existing lining shall be dismantled approximately 200-250 mm beyond the cutout line. q) Wherever the existing refractory is dismantled due to any refractory modification, the same shall be done up to a reasonable extent so that an appropriate refractory application can be carried out. r) Acid Resistance castable shall be replaced with 50% Al2O3 castable as per EIL standard specification 6-79-0029 type C12.

breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION OF REFRACTORY LINING FOR > 5.0 QUALITY ASSURANCE
text: 5.0 QUALITY ASSURANCE

breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION OF REFRACTORY LINING FOR > 5.0 QUALITY ASSURANCE > 5.1 CASTABLE APPLICATION
text: 5.1 CASTABLE APPLICATION

breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION OF REFRACTORY LINING FOR > 5.0 QUALITY ASSURANCE > 5.1 CASTABLE APPLICATION
text:
a) Castable should be installed by vibration in the shuttering. Shuttering should be strong
enough and suitably designed to withstand vibration during application.
b) In case of application by gunning, a procedure shall be established by a mockup in a
45-degree overhead panel, to be witnessed and approved by Client/EIL. Suitable
gunning grade material shall be used.
c) Clean drinking quality water shall be used for mixing of the castable. Water should be
cold enough so that the final mix temperature is maintained within 25 0 C. Water should
have a chloride content less than 200 ppm and a pH of around 6-8. Direct mixing of ice
with water shall be avoided. A suitable water chiller shall be used.

breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION OF REFRACTORY LINING FOR > 5.0 QUALITY ASSURANCE > 5.1 CASTABLE APPLICATION
text: d) Castable lining (cement-bonded) shall be cured using a curing compound. Curing compound shall be as per ASTM C 309, type II class B (resin-based). It shall be applied on castable just after the lining is touch dry. e) During the curing period of 24 hours and preferably for the next 24 hours of natural drying too, there should not be any movement/ rotation of the lined equipment f) The castable mix shall be within a temperature range of 15 °C to 25 °C. Material shall be placed by vibration in the shuttering in panels. Panel joints shall be staggered. g) During actual application, the temperature of the shell plate shall not exceed 32 o C. h) Stoppage and termination of lining shall be as per the established procedure. i) The schedule as specified for air drying, curing, and heat drying shall be ascertained. j) Construction joints of the castable lining shall be staggered. k) Prior to installation of castable lining, the surface shall be thoroughly cleaned. Proper tools and tackles, such as a mixer, vibrator, etc., shall be used for installation. Recommendation, if any, from the manufacturer shall be complied with and adhered to. l) Prior to actual application, mock up shall be carried out for each type of castable resembling the actual application conditions. Test samples shall be taken during each mock-up, which shall be tested for Bulk density (BD) & Cold crushing strength (CCS) after drying at

breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION OF REFRACTORY LINING FOR > 5.0 QUALITY ASSURANCE > 5.1 CASTABLE APPLICATION
text: mock up shall be carried out for each type of castable resembling the actual application conditions. Test samples shall be taken during each mock-up, which shall be tested for Bulk density (BD) & Cold crushing strength (CCS) after drying at 110°C/24 hours and Permanent linear change (PLC) at 800°C (or at maximum specified temp, if value at 800°C is not specified in the approved data sheet of material) as per IS 10570. Mock-up shall be witnessed by the EIL /Client refractory specialist. m) Quality control shall be as per API 936 and 6-79-0007. n) The test results of the mock-up samples shall meet the specification. Parameters maintained in the successful mock-up, i.e., water percentage, mixing time, application methods, etc., shall be maintained during actual application.

breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION OF REFRACTORY LINING FOR > 5.0 QUALITY ASSURANCE > 5.2 BRICK LINING
text: 5.2 BRICK LINING

breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION OF REFRACTORY LINING FOR > 5.0 QUALITY ASSURANCE > 5.2 BRICK LINING
text:
a) There shall be no gap within the lining.
b) Individual cut bricks shall not be less than half of the original size.
c) If required, bricks may be cut to suit at the site. A proper brick-cutting machine with
diamond wheels shall be used for the purpose.
d) After all activities, the top layer bricks of the existing floor lining shall be removed,
cleaned, and re-applied dry (without mortar).

breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION OF REFRACTORY LINING FOR > 5.0 QUALITY ASSURANCE > 5.3 CERAMIC FIBER LINING
text: 5.3 CERAMIC FIBER LINING

breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION OF REFRACTORY LINING FOR > 5.0 QUALITY ASSURANCE > 5.3 CERAMIC FIBER LINING
text:
a) Anchor spacing to be verified as per the approved drawing
b) Anchor welding procedure and type of welding electrodes shall be verified
c) Locking arrangement for the blanket shall be checked for tightness.

breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION OF REFRACTORY LINING FOR > 5.0 QUALITY ASSURANCE > 5.3 CERAMIC FIBER LINING
text:
d) Back-up blanket joints shall have at least 13mm compressed butt joints.
e) At no location, a blanket from the edges shall be left in hanging position. The distance
between the stud and blanket edges shall strictly be maintained
f) Folds shall always be kept towards the hot face side, and the same shall be verified.
g) Module systems shall be installed so that joints at each edge (along the folding direction)
are compressed by 5 mm to avoid gaps due to shrinkage.
h) Hand packing of gaps /voids after module installation is not permitted. If necessary,
modules shall be removed and reinstalled.
i) Compression bands of the CF module shall not be removed until the completion of arch
lining. After removal of the compression bands, tamping shall be done on the hot face of
the CF module to eliminate the gaps.

breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION OF REFRACTORY LINING FOR > 6.0 INSPECTION & TESTING
text: 6.0 INSPECTION & TESTING

breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION OF REFRACTORY LINING FOR > 6.0 INSPECTION & TESTING
text: a) During actual application of castable, test samples shall be taken for each day’s installation and kept in an orderly manner with proper traceability. One set of test samples shall consist of three samples for BD & CCS. The sample shall be selected at random and tested for BD & CCS (after drying at 110°C/24 hours). b) Application of castable lining shall meet the requirements of API RP 936. Testing of castable samples shall be as per the test standards mentioned in the approved datasheet. All testing charges shall be borne by the contractor. Tolerances of insulating castable properties for production specimen: Bulk Density : Maximum - 80 kg/m 3 Minimum - 80 kg/m 3 CCS : Maximum - Not applicable Minimum - 10% PLC : +/- 10% c) Castable-lined items shall be hammer tested for soundness. Tolerance on the width and depth of cracks in castable linings: max. 1.5 mm wide and up to half the lining thickness deep. Beyond this limit, it shall be repaired as per standard procedure. d) Lining thickness and other dimensions of lining shall be checked, and the same shall be within the tolerance limit. e) Physical verification of bricks with reference to shape, size, cracks, broken corners, surface imperfections, etc., shall be carried out. f) The following are the tolerances for the acceptability of fabrication and erection of the heaters applicable for refractory work: Tolerances on castable thickness: -0, +6mm Tolerances on expansion

breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION OF REFRACTORY LINING FOR > 6.0 INSPECTION & TESTING
text: size, cracks, broken corners, surface imperfections, etc., shall be carried out. f) The following are the tolerances for the acceptability of fabrication and erection of the heaters applicable for refractory work: Tolerances on castable thickness: -0, +6mm Tolerances on expansion gap O/S burner tiles: +2.0 mm, -0.0 mm Tolerances on expansion gaps for bricks: +3.0, -0.0 mm

breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION OF REFRACTORY LINING FOR > 6.0 INSPECTION & TESTING
text:
g) Quality of welding strength of anchors shall be tested by randomly striking with a hammer
and bending from the mean position.
h) CF lining shall be inspected for integrity, pattern of modules, thickness, etc.
i) EIL refractory specialist may inspect the ongoing/final application as per the owner’s
instruction. However, the contractor shall depute an experienced refractory
supervisor/inspector during refractory installation and shall report any abnormalities /
submit daily quality log sheets to the owner on a day-to-day basis. Any defects identified
by the owner and/or the owner’s appointed inspector at any stage of application shall be
rectified by the contractor without any time and cost implications.

breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION OF REFRACTORY LINING FOR > 7.0 HSE REQUIREMENT
text: 7.0 HSE REQUIREMENT

breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION OF REFRACTORY LINING FOR > 7.0 HSE REQUIREMENT
text:
a) Contractor shall prepare HSE risk assessment analysis and requirements, which shall
be part of the application procedure. HSE requirements shall be considered for refractory
storage, mixing, application, dry out, anchor welding, etc.
b) Contractor shall follow EIL standard 6-79-0024 for HSE requirements for all the activities
for refractory application.

breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION OF REFRACTORY LINING FOR > 8.0 VENDOR DATA REQUIREMENT
text: 8.0 VENDOR DATA REQUIREMENT

breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION OF REFRACTORY LINING FOR > 8.0 VENDOR DATA REQUIREMENT
text:
Contractor shall submit the following to EIL for review:
a) Refractory product datasheet and compliance datasheet.
b) Refractory modification drawings are to be submitted.
c) Application & dry out procedure, inspection test plan for application
